ideal_architecture.name: Clean Architecture with Dependency Injection
ideal_architecture.description: A layered architecture following Clean Architecture principles with clear separation of concerns, dependency inversion, and use of dependency injection for loose coupling

layers.name: Presentation Layer
responsibilities: Manage UI state and user interactions
Delegate business logic to use cases
dependencies: Should not depend on: Infrastructure Layer, Domain Layer directly

layers.name: Application Layer
responsibilities: Orchestrate flow of data to and from entities
Coordinate use cases through domain services
dependencies: Should not depend on: Infrastructure Layer, Presentation Layer

layers.name: Domain Layer
responsibilities: Define core business objects and their behaviors
Contain domain events and event handlers
dependencies: Should not depend on: Any other layer

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
responsibilities: Implement interfaces defined in inner layers
Handle external concerns like databases, APIs, frameworks
Provide technical capabilities to inner layers
dependencies: Depends on: All inner layers (through interface implementation)
Can depend on: Other infrastructure components

ideal_architecture.dependency_rule: Dependencies point inward: outer layers depend on inner layers, inner layers know nothing about outer layers
ideal_architecture.dependency_injection: Dependencies are injected through constructors or setters, allowing for loose coupling and easy testing

refactoring_blueprint.description: Refactor controllers to be thin adapters that delegate to use cases/services

proposed_changes.change: Introduce Use Case/Service layer between controllers and models
proposed_changes.explanation: Controllers currently contain business logic that should be separated. Create use cases that encapsulate specific business operations.
impact: Easier testing (controllers can be mocked, use cases can be tested independently)
Better maintainability (business logic in one place)
Clearer APIs (use cases have descriptive names)
migration_path: 1. Create use case classes for each major business operation (CreateCampaignUseCase, LaunchCampaignUseCase, etc.)
2. Move business logic from controllers to use cases
3. Controllers become thin adapters that validate input, call use cases, and format output
4. Use dependency injection to provide use cases to controllers
5. Update tests to test use cases directly
proposed_changes.risk_justification: Requires changing many files and ensuring all business logic is correctly moved. However, the logic is well-contained in controllers, making extraction straightforward.

proposed_changes.explanation: Controllers currently directly require models and services. Use dependency injection to break this tight coupling.
impact: Reduced coupling between controllers and implementations
Ability to swap implementations (e.g., different email services)
migration_path: 1. Define interfaces for services (e.g., EmailSenderInterface, DomainVerifierInterface)
2. Modify controllers to accept dependencies through constructor
3. Create implementation classes that satisfy interfaces
4. Update server initialization to inject dependencies
5. Use a simple DI container or manual injection
proposed_changes.risk_justification: Requires changing constructor signatures throughout the codebase. However, JavaScript's flexibility makes this manageable.

refactoring_blueprint.description: Create a proper service layer to encapsulate business logic

proposed_changes.change: Create domain services for core business concepts
proposed_changes.explanation: Extract business logic from controllers and models into dedicated services.
impact: Easier to modify business logic without affecting presentation or data layers
migration_path: 1. Identify domain services: CampaignService, DomainService, UserService, EmailService, WarmupService, TrackingService
2. Move relevant business logic from controllers to these services
3. Services should depend on repositories (interfaces) for data access
4. Implement repository interfaces for data access
5. Inject repositories into services
proposed_changes.risk_justification: This is a major architectural change affecting almost every file. Requires careful planning and execution to ensure business logic is correctly extracted and interfaces are properly defined.

proposed_changes.change: Create repository interfaces for data access
proposed_changes.explanation: Replace direct model usage with repository interfaces to abstract data access.
impact: Ability to swap data sources (e.g., MongoDB to PostgreSQL)
Clearer separation between business logic and data access
migration_path: 1. Define repository interfaces for each aggregate root (ICampaignRepository, IDomainRepository, etc.)
2. Create repository implementations that use Mongoose models
3. Modify services to depend on repository interfaces instead of direct models
4. Update service implementations to use repository methods
5. Inject repositories into services
proposed_changes.risk_justification: Requires changing data access patterns throughout the service layer. However, the current model usage is consistent, making migration systematic.

refactoring_blueprint.description: Refactor infrastructure concerns to follow proper layering

proposed_changes.change: Refactor PostfixLogParser to follow clean architecture principles
proposed_changes.explanation: The PostfixLogParser currently mixes parsing, persistence, buffering, stats, and event emission. Separate these concerns.
impact: Better separation of concerns within infrastructure
Easier to test parsing logic in isolation
Ability to change persistence mechanism without affecting parsing
migration_path: 1. Separate concerns: LogParser (pure parsing), EventBuilder, BufferManager, PersistenceService, StatsTracker, EventEmitter
2. Create interfaces for each concern where appropriate
3. Implement each concern as a separate class
4. Use dependency injection to assemble the parser
5. Ensure each class has a single responsibility
proposed_changes.risk_justification: The PostfixLogParser is a complex class but is relatively isolated. Refactoring it will improve maintainability without affecting many other components.

proposed_changes.explanation: Split RelayPool into GlobalRelayPool and UserTransportFactory to separate global relay management from user-specific transport creation.
impact: Easier to test each concern independently
Ability to modify global relay logic without affecting user transports
migration_path: 1. Create GlobalRelayPool class for managing global Brebo/AWS SES transports
2. Create UserTransportFactory class for creating user-specific nodemailer transports
3. Update wherever RelayPool is used to use the appropriate new class
4. Ensure proper dependency injection if needed
proposed_changes.risk_justification: RelayPool is relatively small and used in limited places. The refactoring is straightforward.

proposed_changes.change: Extract configuration to proper configuration management
proposed_changes.explanation: Move hard-coded values to configuration files or environment variables.
impact: Reduced magic numbers and strings in code
migration_path: 1. Create configuration module that loads from environment variables
2. Identify all hard-coded values that should be configurable
3. Replace hard-coded values with configuration references
4. Provide default values for development
5. Document required environment variables
proposed_changes.risk_justification: Configuration extraction is straightforward and low-risk, though it requires identifying all the hard-coded values.

refactoring_blueprint.description: Improve frontend architecture to follow better React practices

proposed_changes.change: Implement proper state management with Context API or state management library
proposed_changes.explanation: App.jsx currently manages too much state and passes too many props. Use React Context for global state.
impact: Easier to reason about state changes
migration_path: 1. Identify global state pieces (auth state, domain state, campaign state, toast state, etc.)
2. Create context providers for each domain (AuthContext, DashboardContext, etc.)
3. Move state management logic to context providers
4. Consume context in components instead of receiving props
5. Keep App.jsx as a simple layout component
proposed_changes.risk_justification: Requires identifying all state usage and creating appropriate contexts. However, React Context is well-established and low-risk to implement.

proposed_changes.change: Extract business logic from components to service/hooks
proposed_changes.explanation: Components currently contain business logic for data transformation and API interaction patterns.
impact: Better separation of concerns in frontend
migration_path: 1. Create custom hooks for common API interaction patterns (useCampaigns, useDomains, etc.)
2. Move data transformation logic to utility functions
3. Components focus on UI and delegate logic to hooks/services
4. Ensure hooks are testable in isolation
proposed_changes.risk_justification: Extracting logic to hooks is a standard React practice and carries low risk.

proposed_changes.change: Implement proper error boundaries and loading states
proposed_changes.explanation: Current error handling is inconsistent and loading states are not always properly managed.
impact: Better user experience during errors and loading
migration_path: 1. Create ErrorBoundary component for catching runtime errors
2. Create LoadingSpinner or Skeleton components for consistent loading states
3. Wrap async data fetching in components with proper loading/error states
4. Use React Suspense where appropriate for code splitting
proposed_changes.risk_justification: Adding error boundaries and loading states is straightforward and improves robustness.

refactoring_blueprint.description: Better handle cross-cutting concerns like logging, validation, and error handling

proposed_changes.explanation: Replace repetitive try/catch blocks in controllers with centralized error handling.
impact: Easier to modify error handling logic
migration_path: 1. Create error handling middleware that catches async errors
2. Move specific error handling logic (like 11000 duplicate key) to this middleware
3. Remove try/catch blocks from individual controller methods
4. Ensure middleware is placed correctly in the Express chain
proposed_changes.risk_justification: Express middleware for error handling is well-established and low-risk to implement.

proposed_changes.explanation: Centralize validation logic to avoid duplication and ensure consistency.
migration_path: 1. Create validation functions for common patterns (email, ID, required fields, etc.)
2. Create validation middleware that can be applied to routes
3. Replace ad-hoc validation in controllers with validation service calls
4. Ensure validation errors are formatted consistently
proposed_changes.risk_justification: Validation extraction is straightforward and commonly done in Node.js applications.

proposed_changes.explanation: Standardize response formatting across all endpoints.
migration_path: 1. Create successResponse and errorResponse helper functions
2. Replace direct res.json() and res.status().json() calls with helpers
3. Ensure consistent status codes and response structure
4. Consider creating a response formatting middleware
proposed_changes.risk_justification: Creating helper functions is trivial and carries almost no risk.

implementation_roadmap.description: Establish foundational improvements that enable safer refactoring
tasks: Create configuration management system
Extract validation helpers
Create response formatting helpers
Implement centralized error handling middleware
Set up dependency injection pattern (simple constructor injection)
Write comprehensive tests for existing functionality (to ensure safety during refactoring)
implementation_roadmap.goal: Create a stable foundation with reduced duplication and better error handling

implementation_roadmap.description: Introduce service layer and begin separating concerns
tasks: Create repository interfaces for major aggregates
Create repository implementations using Mongoose
Identify and create domain services (starting with least coupled)
Begin moving business logic from controllers to services
Update controllers to use services instead of direct model access
Update tests to use service layer
implementation_roadmap.goal: Establish clean separation between presentation, application, and domain layers

implementation_roadmap.description: Refactor infrastructure components to follow proper layering
tasks: Refactor PostfixLogParser to separate concerns
Refactor RelayPool into GlobalRelayPool and UserTransportFactory
Ensure all infrastructure depends on interfaces from inner layers
Update any remaining direct infrastructure usage in domain/application layers
Test infrastructure components in isolation
implementation_roadmap.goal: Have infrastructure properly implementing interfaces from inner layers

implementation_roadmap.description: Improve frontend architecture and state management
tasks: Implement React Context for global state management
Extract business logic to custom hooks and utilities
Implement error boundaries and loading states
Refactor components to use context and hooks
Test frontend components thoroughly
implementation_roadmap.goal: Have a clean, maintainable frontend with proper state separation

implementation_roadmap.description: Final optimization, cleanup, and quality improvements
tasks: Performance profiling and optimization
Code cleanup and standardization
Documentation updates
Final comprehensive testing
Prepare for deployment

success_criteria: All business logic is testable in isolation
Controllers are thin adapters with no business logic
Services depend only on interfaces, not implementations
Infrastructure implements interfaces from inner layers
Frontend has clear separation between UI and business logic
No God objects or overly complex components
Consistent error handling, validation, and response formatting
Easy to modify or replace individual components
